html:
Exclusive to:

Kelp Fronds are a Vegetable Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are harvested from Bull Kelp plants (in the Ocean and in the Rocky Beach Biome) and from Ocean Debris. When eaten, they restore a bit of Hunger, but reduce both the player's Health and Sanity.

Kelp Fronds can be dried on Drying Racks to acquire Dried Kelp Fronds and can be used in the Crock Pot. If dried on Large Drying Rack, they will give additional Salt Crystals after drying. They are also required to cook California Rolls, to craft the Seawreath and to construct the Royal Tapestry.

Cooked

Cooked Kelp Fronds are a Vegetable food item acquired by cooking Kelp Fronds on Fire. They do not deduct Health unlike their raw counterpart.

Dried

Dried Kelp Fronds are a Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. It is produced by drying Kelp Fronds on a Drying Rack for a quarter of a Day. It is good to quickly restore sanity due to its accessibility and long spoil time. It can also be a good ingredient to be used in the Crock Pot as a vegetable, except it cannot be used to cook dishes which require a high vegetable value.

Dried Kelp Fronds restore 1 , 9.375 , 10 , and last 20 Days. It has 0.5 vegetable value in the Crock Pot.

Usage

Recipes

×2

×2

Trivia

  • Kelp Fronds were introduced in the Turn of Tides update. The ability to add them to the Crock Pot was introduced in the Hook, Line, and Inker update.
  • Kelp Fronds are one of the few non-Meat Items that can be dried by the player, the others being Seaweed in the Shipwrecked DLC and the Flytrap Stalk in the Hamlet DLC.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

"kelp"
“It gets my pockets all wet and gross.”
–Wilson, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“HEY! Who dares me to eat it raw?”
–Willow, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Slimy sea spinach!”
–Wolfgang, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“It's... I don't know. Sigh.”
–Wendy, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“I STOLE IT FROM THE HORRIBLE WATER”
–WX-78, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“It is an edible kelp species.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Could cook it up real nice.”
–Woodie, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Can I eat something that's not slimy for once?”
–Maxwell, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Ridiculöus sea vegetable.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Slimy, yet satisfying.”
–Webber, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Salad of the sea!”
–Warly, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Belly stuff?”
–Wormwood, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“I'll try anything once.”
–Winona, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Kelp would be of no help.”
–Wortox, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Sea snack!”
–Wurt, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“Sea monster hair. Obviously.”
–Walter, when examining Kelp Fronds.
“It tastes about as good as it looks.”
–Wanda, when examining Kelp Fronds.
Kelp Fronds are a Vegetable Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together, introduced in Return of Them. They are harvested from Bull Kelp plants (in the Ocean and in the Rocky Beach Biome) and from Ocean Debris. When eaten, they restore a bit of Hunger, but reduce both the player's Health and Sanity.
Kelp Fronds can be dried on Drying Racks to acquire Dried Kelp Fronds and can be used in the Crock Pot.
If dried on Large Drying Rack, they will give additional Salt Crystals after drying. They are also required to cook California Rolls, to craft the Seawreath and to construct the Royal Tapestry.

Cooked Kelp Fronds

9.375
0

Object Info

Can be Burned
Miscellaneous
"kelp_cooked"
“It's closer to a liquid than a solid.”
–Wilson, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Great, now I don't have to chew.”
–Willow, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Sea spinach make Wolfgang strong to the finish.”
–Wolfgang, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“The things we do for sustenance.”
–Wendy, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“ENERGY SLOP”
–WX-78, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“That certainly didn't improve the texture.”
–Wickerbottom, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Good eatin' right there, if you ask me.”
–Woodie, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“This was not an improvement.”
–Maxwell, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Tis even wörse than beföre.”
–Wigfrid, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“He doesn't like it very much, but I think it's okay.”
–Webber, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“This... could be a bit better.”
–Warly, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Mmmm... slimy!”
–Wormwood, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Well, that's not how I would have prepared it.”
–Winona, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Mortals have such funny tastes.”
–Wortox, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“Mmmm, slimy!”
–Wurt, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“It's... slimy.”
–Walter, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
“I don't know why I wasted time cooking it.”
–Wanda, when examining Cooked Kelp Fronds.
Cooked Kelp Fronds are a Vegetable food item acquired by cooking Kelp Fronds on Fire. They do not deduct Health unlike their raw counterpart.

“The sodium content's kinda high.”
–Wilson
“It's not half bad.”
–Willow
“Little sea flakes make Wolfgang grow strong!”
–Wolfgang
“We took it out of the ocean, then took the ocean out of it.”
–Wendy
“IT TASTES LIKE NOTHING”
–WX-78
“It's slightly more palatable with this texture.”
–Wickerbottom
“It's a nice light snack.”
–Woodie
“Sadly, I've eaten worse out here.”
–Maxwell
“I despise it.”
–Wigfrid
“Mmm, salty and flaky!”
–Webber
“Ah, crispy!”
–Warly
“Crunchy”
–Wormwood
“I kinda like the stuff!”
–Winona
“I do not want it, no siree.”
–Wortox
“Salty crunchies.”
–Wurt
“Crunchy and salty. It's not bad.”
–Walter
“I don't know if it was worth the wait.”
–Wanda

'''Kelp Fronds''' are a [[Vegetable]] [[Food]] [[Item]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. They are harvested from [[Bull Kelp]] plants (in the [[Ocean]] and in the [[Rocky Beach]] [[Biomes|Biome]]) and from [[Ocean Debris]].  When eaten, they restore a bit of [[Hunger]], but reduce both the player's [[Health]] and [[Sanity]].

Kelp Fronds can be dried on [[Drying Rack]]s to acquire [[Dried Kelp Fronds]] and can be used in the [[Crock Pot]]. 
If dried on [[Large Drying Rack]], they will give additional [[Salt Crystals]] after drying. They are also required to cook [[California Roll]]s, to craft the [[Seawreath]] and to construct the [[Royal Tapestry]].

'''Dried Kelp Fronds''' are a [[Food]] [[Item]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'', introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. It is produced by drying [[Kelp Fronds]] on a [[Drying Rack]] for a quarter of a [[Day]]. It is good to quickly restore sanity due to its accessibility and long spoil time. It can also be a good ingredient to be used in the [[Crock Pot]] as a vegetable, except it cannot be used to cook dishes which require a high vegetable value.

Dried Kelp Fronds restore 1 {{Hp}}, 9.375 {{Hunger}}, 10 {{Sanity}}, and last 20 Days. It has 0.5 vegetable value in the Crock Pot.

=={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia==
* Kelp Fronds were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Turn of Tides|Turn of Tides]]'' update. The ability to add them to the Crock Pot was introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Hook, Line, and Inker|Hook, Line, and Inker]]'' update.

* Kelp Fronds are one of the few non-[[Meats|Meat]] Items that can be dried by the player, the others being [[Seaweed]] in the ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLC and the [[Flytrap Stalk]] in the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC.
